4. Click Send to test email
Expected Result: Notification email sent successfully using default template

Title: Notification Attachment — No Attachment
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Notification template exists without attachments
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/notifications
2. Select a notification type that has no attachments configured
3. Open the notification template details blade
4. Verify the Attachments widget or section is visible
5. Verify the attachment list is empty
6. Verify the UI indicates the no-attachment state clearly (e.g., empty state message)
7. Send a test notification from this template
8. Verify the received email has no file attachments
Expected Result:
- Attachments section is visible in the notification template blade
- Empty attachment state is displayed with appropriate messaging
- No attachment files are listed when none are configured
- Test email sent from this template arrives without any file attachments
- No error is shown for templates without attachments

Title: Notification Attachment Widget — Upload Attachment
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Notification template exists
PDF or image file available for upload
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/notifications
2. Open a notification template details blade
3. Locate the Attachments widget or section
4. Click Add or Upload to attach a file
5. Select a PDF or image file from the file picker
6. Verify the file appears in the attachments list
7. Save the template
8. Send a test notification
9. Verify the received email includes the uploaded file as an attachment
Expected Result:
- Attachments widget is visible on the notification template blade
- File upload dialog opens when clicking the upload button
- Uploaded file appears in the attachment list with filename and size
- Template saves successfully with the attachment
- Test email is received with the file correctly attached
- Attachment file is downloadable and uncorrupted in the received email

Title: Notification Preview with Sample Data
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Notification template exists with dynamic fields (e.g., {CustomerName}, {OrderNumber})
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/notifications
2. Select a notification type with dynamic template fields
3. Open the template and click Preview
4. Enter sample data values for each dynamic field (e.g., CustomerName: John Doe)
5. Click the Preview button to render the template
6. Verify all dynamic placeholders are replaced with sample values
7. Modify one sample data value
8. Click Preview again and verify the output updates with the new value
Expected Result:
- Preview option is accessible from the notification template blade
- Sample data form shows all dynamic fields available in the template
- Entering sample data and clicking Preview renders the template with substituted values
- All placeholders (e.g., {CustomerName}) are replaced with the entered sample data
- Preview output is visually accurate to how the email will appear to recipients
- Changing sample data and re-previewing updates the rendered output correctly

Title: Notification Preview — Email Format
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Email notification template exists with HTML content
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/notifications
2. Select an email notification type
3. Open the template blade
4. Click Preview
5. Enter sample data for required dynamic fields
6. Click Preview to render
7. Verify the preview displays in HTML email format (not plain text)
8. Verify any layout template (header/footer) is applied
9. Verify images and styling render correctly in the preview
Expected Result:
- Email notification preview renders in HTML format with proper email structure
- Preview includes any assigned notification layout (header, footer, branding)
- Images embedded in the template render correctly in the preview
- Email styling (fonts, colors, spacing) is applied consistently
- Preview accurately represents the email appearance in a mail client
- Plain text fallback is noted if the template includes one

Title: Share Preview via Email — Send Preview to Recipient
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Notification preview has been rendered with sample data
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/notifications and open a notification template
2. Click Preview and enter sample data
3. Click Preview to render the template
4. Click the Share or Send Preview button
5. Enter a valid email address in the recipient field
6. Click Send to deliver the preview email
7. Check the recipient email inbox
8. Verify the email is received with the preview content
Expected Result:
- Share or Send Preview button is available on the rendered preview page
- Recipient email address field accepts valid email format
- Clicking Send dispatches the preview email to the specified address
- Preview email is received in the inbox with correct template content
- Sample data values appear substituted correctly in the received email
- Email subject and sender information are correctly populated

Title: Abandoned Cart Notification — Enable Setting
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Notification module installed
Abandoned cart feature available in Settings
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/settings
2. Open the Notifications section or Abandoned Cart settings
3. Locate the Enable Abandoned Cart Notifications toggle
4. Set the toggle to Enabled
5. Save the settings
6. Verify the setting is saved as enabled
7. Create a cart on the storefront and abandon it without checking out
8. Wait for the configured reminder period
9. Verify the abandoned cart notification email is sent
Expected Result:
- Abandoned Cart Notifications enable toggle is available in Settings
- Setting is saved successfully when enabled
- System recognizes abandoned carts after the configured delay period
- Abandoned cart email notification is sent to the customer who abandoned the cart
- Notification content includes cart items and a link to resume checkout

Title: Abandoned Cart — Delay Configuration
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Abandoned cart notifications are enabled [NOTIF-058]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/settings and open Abandoned Cart settings
2. Locate the delay or reminder interval configuration field
3. Change the delay to a shorter period (e.g., 1 hour or minimum allowed value)
4. Save the setting
5. Create and abandon a cart on the storefront
6. Wait for the configured delay period
7. Verify the abandoned cart email is sent after the configured delay
8. Change delay to a longer period and verify the email is sent at the new interval
Expected Result:
- Delay configuration field is visible and editable in the abandoned cart settings
- Delay value is saved correctly after clicking Save
- Abandoned cart notification is triggered after the configured delay period (not before)
- Changing the delay value takes effect for newly abandoned carts
- Email timestamp confirms the notification was sent at the correct delay interval
- Minimum and maximum delay values are enforced by validation

Title: Abandoned Cart — Template Content
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Abandoned cart notifications are enabled [NOTIF-058]
Customer has an abandoned cart with multiple items
Steps:
1. Open the storefront as a registered customer
2. Add at least 2 different products to the cart
3. Navigate away from the checkout without completing the purchase
4. Wait for the abandoned cart notification delay to expire
5. Check the customer email inbox
6. Open the abandoned cart notification email
7. Verify the email includes all abandoned cart items with names and quantities
8. Verify a link or button to resume checkout is present in the email
Expected Result:
- Abandoned cart email is received at the customer email address
- Email subject line references the abandoned cart or order
- All items added to the cart are listed in the email with product names and quantities
- Product images are included in the email if the template supports it
- A clear call-to-action button or link to resume checkout is included
- Cart link in the email restores the cart session correctly when clicked

Title: Abandoned Cart — Disable Setting
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Abandoned cart notifications are currently enabled [NOTIF-058]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/settings and open the Abandoned Cart settings
2. Set the Enable Abandoned Cart Notifications toggle to Disabled
3. Save the setting
4. Verify the setting is saved as disabled
5. Create and abandon a cart on the storefront
6. Wait for the abandoned cart delay period to expire
7. Check the customer email inbox
8. Verify NO abandoned cart notification email is received
9. Re-enable the setting and verify notifications resume
Expected Result:
- Abandoned Cart Notifications toggle can be disabled and saved correctly
- After disabling, no abandoned cart emails are sent even after the delay period expires
- Customer email inbox does not receive abandoned cart notification when feature is disabled
- Re-enabling the setting restores abandoned cart notification behavior for new carts
- Disabling the setting does not affect other notification types

Title: Notification Layout CRUD — Create Notification Layout
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Notification module installed
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/notifications
2. Open the Notification Layouts section
3. Click Add to create a new layout
4. Enter a layout name (e.g., Company Email Layout)
5. Enter HTML template content with header and footer sections
6. Click Save to create the layout
7. Verify the layout appears in the layouts list
8. Open the layout again to verify all content is saved correctly
Expected Result:

- Add button allows creating a new layout
- Layout name and HTML template content are accepted and saved
- Newly created layout appears in the layouts list with the correct name
- Reopening the layout shows all saved content without data loss

Title: Notification Layout — Edit and Preview
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Notification layout exists [NOTIF-062]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/notifications and open Notification Layouts
2. Open an existing notification layout
3. Modify the HTML content (e.g., change the header color or add footer text)
4. Click Save to persist the changes
5. Assign the layout to a notification template
6. Open the notification template and click Preview
7. Render a preview with sample data
8. Verify the preview shows the layout wrapper with the updated content
Expected Result:
- Notification layout HTML content is editable after initial creation
- Changes to the layout are saved and persisted correctly
- Layout modification is reflected in all notification templates using this layout
- Preview of a notification template using this layout shows the updated layout design
- Header and footer sections from the layout wrap the notification body content
- Layout preview renders HTML correctly without broken styling

Title: Password Reset Notification Limit — Verify Rate Limiting
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
User account exists with valid email
Password reset rate limiting is configured in settings
Steps:
1. Navigate to the storefront or admin login page
2. Request a password reset for a user account
3. Verify the first password reset email is received
4. Immediately request a second password reset for the same account
5. Verify the second request is accepted or rate-limited depending on configuration
6. Continue requesting password resets to exceed the configured limit
7. Verify that additional requests are blocked with an appropriate error message
8. Verify that blocked requests do not send additional emails
Expected Result:
- First password reset request generates and sends a reset email successfully
- After exceeding the configured request limit, additional requests are blocked
- Rate limit error message is displayed to the user when limit is exceeded
- No additional password reset emails are sent once the limit is reached
- Rate limiting window resets after the configured cooldown period
- Admin can view and manage rate limit settings in notification configuration
